_comment: Single source of truth for build-loop's two-axis model taxonomy. SEGMENT axis = work role / primary output; CAPABILITY-TIER axis = a 7-rung ladder. Read by scripts/model_taxonomy.py (the loader every other script imports). Anthropic/OpenAI ids are EXAMPLE SEEDS held as data, NOT hard-codes — classification appends new models to the runtime cache, never to this file. Provider names are illustrative. KISS/DRY: nothing here is duplicated in code.

fallback._comment: One-edge capability fallback walk on the GENERATIVE ladder T0..T5. T-S is specialist (off-ladder): it never participates in the capability fallback walk. The HARD FLOOR INVARIANT is enforced in code: a tier walks AT MOST one edge upward in capability, so T1/frontier never resolves below T2/thinking.

legacy_aliases._comment: The 4 legacy tier tokens map onto ladder rungs. Back-compat: config modelOverrides, plan tier: frontmatter, route_decision, and every existing test reference these tokens — normalize_tier folds them to the ladder so they keep resolving to the same models.

generative_reasoning.label: Generative Reasoning
generative_reasoning._status_note: live resolver consumer — the implicit segment of the legacy tier tokens

representation_retrieval.label: Representation/Retrieval
representation_retrieval._status_note: embeddings used by debugging-memory; no agent-dispatch resolver

realtime_interaction.label: Realtime Interaction
realtime_interaction._status_note: DATA + reference only — no resolver wiring; future skills may consume

perception_input.label: Perception/Input Understanding
perception_input._status_note: DATA + reference only — no resolver wiring

generative_media.label: Generative Media
generative_media._status_note: DATA + reference only — no resolver wiring

preferred._comment: Ordered preferred-model list per (segment, tier). Order = capability rank, honoring Accuracy>Speed>Cost. The resolver picks the highest-ranked AVAILABLE + host-reachable id; ties / equal-or-unranked broken by release recency (newer wins). Users reorder via .build-loop/config.json. ALL 7 segments carry at least one cell so the data shape is uniform; dormant cells are data only — no resolver walks them yet.

models._comment: Seed registry: model id -> {provider, label, segment, tier, tags[], released (ISO date, for the recency tiebreak), status, aliases[]}. status: default (the segment/tier's host fallback) | verified (cross-vendor, advisory) | local | specialist. Runtime classification writes to .build-loop/model-tier-cache.json, NOT this file.

classification_rubric._comment: Segment-appropriate benchmark hints the host LLM uses when classifying an unseen model into BOTH segment and tier. Generative segments grade on reasoning/coding benchmarks; specialist segments grade on their own metrics (recall/NDCG/WER/latency), NOT SWE-bench.
classification_rubric.generative_reasoning: SWE-bench Verified / ARC-AGI / GPQA Diamond — tier by where it lands vs the prior-gen frontier ceiling
classification_rubric.agentic_execution: SWE-bench Verified + tool-use accuracy + tau-bench / agentic-harness scores
classification_rubric.governance_evaluation: judge-agreement rate / classification F1 / moderation precision-recall
classification_rubric.realtime_interaction: WER (word error rate) / end-to-end latency ms / streaming stability
classification_rubric.perception_input: document-VQA / vision / audio / video understanding benchmarks
classification_rubric.primary_role_rule: A model with image/audio INPUT but whose PRIMARY job is reasoning is generative_reasoning with a multimodal-input TAG. Classify into perception_input / realtime_interaction / generative_media ONLY when that IS the primary product role.
classification_rubric.tag_axis: Tags are a third axis (orthogonal to segment+tier): long-context, agentic, multimodal-input, cost-sensitive, restricted, local, coding, fast, nano, edge.
